title: [Phase 8a] looper-api: axum REST server, auth, envelope
description:
REST API crate using axum. Implements the management HTTP API with auth middleware, SSE streaming, and structured error responses.

**Spec refs:** module8-api-webhook.md (API sections), module-testing-strategy.md (golden file tests)

**Key types:**
- `Handler` struct (holds RuntimeState trait + Config)
- `Server` struct (axum Router + listener)
- `RuntimeState` trait (methods: Status, Projects, Loops, Runs, Events, ReconcileStale, WebhookStatus)
- `Envelope<T>` struct with ok: bool, data: Option<T>, error: Option<ErrorInfo>, request_id: String
- `ErrorInfo` — code (ErrorCode enum, 19 variants), message, details
- `ErrorCode` enum — UPPER_SNAKE_CASE (ACTIVE_RUN_NOT_FOUND, AUTH_MISCONFIGURED, etc.) với HTTP status mapping

**Endpoints (31+):**
- Health: GET /api/v1/healthz, /api/v1/status, /api/v1/version, /api/v1/config
- Projects: GET /api/v1/projects, POST /api/v1/projects, DELETE /api/v1/projects/{id}
- Loops: GET /api/v1/loops, POST /api/v1/loops, GET /api/v1/loops/{id}, POST pause/start/retry/stop/close
- Runs: GET /api/v1/runs, POST /api/v1/runs/reconcile-stale (POST only), GET /api/v1/runs/active, GET /api/v1/runs/{id}
- PRs: GET /api/v1/pull-requests, GET /api/v1/pull-requests/{id}
- Webhook: POST /webhook/forward (loopback-only)
- Events: GET /api/v1/events, GET /api/v1/events/{entityType}/{id}
- SSE: GET /api/v1/loops/{id}/logs/follow (channel subscription model)
- Manual: POST /api/v1/workers, POST /api/v1/planners, GET /api/v1/reviewer/repair

**Auth middleware:** authorizeRequest() — loopback exception for webhook, Bearer token for API, auth_mode none for unrestricted. 19 ErrorCode values with mapped HTTP status codes.

**Dep:** looper-types, looper-config, looper-storage, looper-service, looper-scheduler

**Go source reference (legacy):** legacy/internal/api/handler.go (5540 lines) + server.go + legacy/pkg/api/envelope.go

title: Phase 13a — E2E test harness: fake GitHub server + fake agent + fake osascript
description:
Port the Go E2E test harness (internal/e2e/harness/) to Rust. This is the #1 priority because without it you cannot verify any other gap fix works.

Go source: legacy/internal/e2e/harness/ (2,935 LOC)
- harness/config.go — test configuration
- harness/daemon.go — daemon lifecycle management
- harness/git.go — git helpers for tests
- harness/assertions.go — structured test assertions
- harness/artifacts.go — test artifact collection
- harness/ports.go — port management for test servers
- harness/temp_home.go — temp home directory creation
- harness/binaries.go — binary path resolution
- harness/fake_agent.go — fake agent binary manager
- harness/fake_gh.go — fake gh CLI binary manager
- cmd/fake-agent/main.go (12K) — mock AI agent with deterministic output
- cmd/fake-gh/main.go (36K) — mock gh CLI responding to pr view, issue list, etc.
- cmd/fake-osascript/main.go (130 LOC) — capture notification calls

Key design decisions:
- fake-gh must parse real gh CLI args and respond with realistic JSON
- fake-agent must produce deterministic output matching real agent patterns
- Harness manages full daemon lifecycle: start → run → stop
- Works with temp sandbox git repos and SQLite databases

Dependencies on existing Rust code:
- Uses looper-storage repos for DB
- Uses looper-config for test config
- Uses looperd main for daemon bootstrap
- Will become the integration point for all other gap fixes

Existing Go E2E tests that must pass:
- smoke_daemon_test.go — daemon starts, health endpoint responds
- resolve_comments_scenarios_test.go (29K) — review thread resolution
- github_sandbox_test.go (24K) — full GitHub interaction cycle
- dependency_gate_sandbox_test.go (14K) — dependency gate behavior
- invariant_worktree_test.go (16K) — worktree invariants
- githubcontract/contract_test.go (14K) — GitHub API contract tests

This bead blocks ALL other gap fixes because you cannot verify them without E2E tests.

title: [Phase 1] looper-types: Domain enums, state machines, core types
description:
Implement the zero-dependency domain types crate. This is the foundation crate that ALL other crates depend on. It defines pure types with no IO.

**Spec refs:** module-domain-state-machine.md, module1-config-types.md (enum parts only)

**Key Rust types:**
- `LoopType` enum (planner, reviewer, worker, fixer)
- `LoopTargetType` enum (project, pull_request, issue)
- `LoopStatus` enum (10 states: idle, queued, running, paused, waiting, stopped, terminated, completed, failed, interrupted) with transition method `can_transition_to() -> bool`
- `RunStatus` enum (7 states: queued, running, success, failed, cancelled, interrupted, parse_failed) with transition method
- `LoopTarget` struct with `target_key()` method
- `ResumePolicy` enum (6 values: replay_step, retry_from_timeout_context, advance_from_checkpoint, restart_from_discover, manual_intervention, rerun_review)
- `FailureKind` enum (retryable_transient, retryable_after_resume, non_retryable, manual_intervention)
- `FailureBoundary` enum (10+ boundaries: git_remote, git_local, github_api, model_provider, agent_process, local_worktree, storage, config, checkpoint, policy, unknown)
- Agent vendor enums, log level, auth mode, all string enums from config types
- Config structs (ServerConfig, StorageConfig, SchedulerConfig, etc.) — pure data types only
- `PartialConfig` — all-Option version of Config for merge
- `LoopSummary`, `StepName` validation
- `AgentExecutionRecord`, `EventLogRecord`, `QueueItemRecord` — core record types

**Behaviors to implement:**
- `LoopStatus::can_transition_to(target)` — compile-time safe transition matrix (25 valid transitions)
- `RunStatus::can_transition_to(target)` — 7 valid transitions
- `LoopTarget::target_key()` — format: "project:{id}", "issue:{repo}:{number}", "pr:{repo}:{number}"
- `PRLockKey(repo, pr_number)` — "pr:{repo}:{number}"
- `assert_loop_type_matches_target()` — planner→issue, reviewer/fixer→PR, worker→any
- `is_active_loop_status()`, `is_conflicting_loop_status()`, `is_terminal_run_status()`
- `Steps::for_loop_type()` — returns step list for planner/reviewer/fixer/worker

**Test specs:** module-testing-strategy.md (Layer 1 — Unit tests)

**Verification:** cargo test passes with 100% state machine tests

**Go source reference (legacy):** legacy/internal/domain/domain.go, legacy/internal/config/types.go

title: [Phase 2] looper-config: Config loading, validation, merge, disclosure
description:
Config loading crate — 3-layer merge pipeline: defaults → file (TOML/YAML/JSON) → env vars → CLI flags.

**Spec refs:** module1-config-types.md (full), module-security-concurrency.md (env sanitization)

**Key types:**
- `Config` (top-level) — all sub-configs merged
- `PartialConfig` — all-Option version for incremental merge
- `ConfigLoader` — builder pattern, load from file/env/cli
- `ConfigValidationError` — field-level issues list
- `ValidationIssue { path, message }`
- `LoadFileMetadata { config_path, config_file_present, tool_detection }`
- `Stamper` — disclosure stamp builder

**Behaviors:**
- `resolve_config_path()` — search ~/.looper/config.{toml,yaml,yml,json}
- `load_file()` → merge defaults → file → env → CLI
- `validate()` — 300+ validation rules per field (port range, enum values, path writable, etc.)
- `normalize()` — apply defaults to partial
- `merge_config()` / `merge_*_config()` — deep merge for each sub-config
- `build_env_overrides()` — 50+ env var mappings
- Disclosure stamper: `stamp_commit_message()`, `stamp_markdown()`, `strip_markdown_stamp()`, `has_markdown_stamp()`
- `Stamper::from_config()` with version, agent, model info
- Protected instruction phrase detection (30+ blocked phrases)

**Dependencies:** looper-types for enums and struct definitions

**Tests:** 300+ validation rule tests, format roundtrip (TOML→YAML→JSON), env var mapping, disclosure stamping, mixed schema detection

**Verification:** All config formats parse correctly, validation catches all error cases

**Go source reference (legacy):** legacy/internal/config/ (load.go, validate.go, normalize.go, defaults.go)

title: [Phase 5a] looper-github: GitHub gateway via gh CLI
description:
GitHub integration gateway wrapping gh CLI commands. The largest IO crate in terms of API surface.

**Spec refs:** module3-github-gateway.md, module8-api-webhook.md (webhook routing sections)

**Key types:**
- `Gateway` struct (gh_path, cwd, discovery_cache, gh_run fn, review_submit_diagnostic fn)
- `GatewayOptions` — constructor config with gh_path, cwd, discovery_cache_ttl, gh_run override
- `PullRequestSummary` / `PullRequestDetail` — parsed from gh CLI JSON output
- `IssueSummary` / `IssueDetail` — parsed from gh issue JSON
- `SubmitReviewInput` — with inline comments, anchors, disclosure config
- `ReviewThread`, `ReviewComment`, `ReviewMarkerResult`
- `DiscoveryPullRequestListCacheEntry` / `DiscoveryIssueListCacheEntry` (TTL 30s)
- `TransientError` — wraps transient gh errors for retry logic

**Behaviors (50+ gh CLI patterns):**
- PR ops: list (label/author/base filters), view (full detail), diff, create, close, auto-merge, edit title/body
- Issue ops: list, view, comments, timeline, reactions, create/update/delete comments, close, labels, assignees
- Review ops: submit (inline comments via REST API, quality gate, disclosure), find marker, threads (list/resolve/reply)
- Auth: gh auth status, get current user, get repo permission, detect repo
- Labels: init standard looper labels (looper:plan, looper:spec-reviewing, etc.)
- Transient error detection: pattern matching (tls timeout, 502, 503, 504, rate limit, etc.)
- Discovery cache: 3 caches (PR, review-PR, issues) with TTL and mutex guard

**Key function:** submit_review() — most complex: diagnoses request, checks idempotency, normalizes anchors, runs quality gate, normalizes disclosure, submits via REST API or gh CLI fallback

**Dep:** looper-types, looper-config

**Go source reference (legacy):** legacy/internal/infra/github/gateway.go (3694 lines) + errors.go

title: [Phase 6] looper-scheduler: Tick loop, queue claim, discovery, recovery
description:
Scheduler crate — runs the discovery and queue claim loops. This is the heartbeat of the daemon.

Spec refs: module6-scheduler.md, module-recovery-infra.md (recovery pipeline)

Key types: Scheduler (tick_interval, wakeCh, stopCh, ticker), ActiveExecutionRegistry, AsyncRunner trait, HandlerMap, QueuePriority constants

Behaviors:
- Two concurrent loops: main tick loop (configurable interval, default 30s) + claim pump (1s)
- Tick flow: pre-claim → project iteration (discovery per role) → post-claim
- Discovery per role: Planner (issues with looper:plan label), Reviewer (PRs with review requested), Fixer (PRs with fix items), Worker (issues with looper:worker-ready), Coordinator (triage + dispatch)
- Queue claim: compute available_slots, claim non-long-term-retry first, then long-term-retry
- Claim SQL: CTE UPDATE ... RETURNING with lock key + blocker exclusion
- Stale reconciliation: when slots==0, reconcile stale running runs
- Wake channel sources: webhook received, CLI action, recovery requeue, new project
- Recovery pipeline (5 phases on startup):
  1. Orphan agent cleanup (PID verification via ps)
  2. Expired lock release
  3. Stale run reconciliation (30min heartbeat threshold)
  4. Loop state normalization
  5. Reviewer auto-recovery (deferred background)
- Failure classification: 4 FailureKind × 10 FailureBoundary mapping
- Long-term retry bucket: attempts >= 5 in slow lane

Dep: looper-types, looper-storage, looper-service, looper-github, looper-git, looper-agent

**Go source reference (legacy):** legacy/internal/runtime/scheduler.go (1757 lines) + legacy/internal/scheduler/scheduler.go

title: Phase 13i — Release manifest builder: artifact collection + SHA256 + version compatibility
description:
Port the release manifest builder from Go internal/release/ (207 LOC).

Go source: legacy/internal/release/manifest.go

Key types:
- Manifest: ManifestVersion, Version, Tag, Released, Channel, APIVersion, SchemaVersion, MinCliForDaemon, MinDaemonForCli, Artifacts map[string]Artifact
- Artifact: URL, SHA256, Size
- BuildManifestInput: Tag, Version, Released, Channel, APIVersion, SchemaVersion, MinCliForDaemon, MinDaemonForCli, Repo, AssetsDir, RequiredArtifacts

Key functions:
- BuildManifest(input) -> Manifest — validate input, collect artifacts, build manifest
- EncodeManifest(manifest) -> []byte — pretty-printed JSON
- CurrentSchemaVersion() -> string — latest migration ID from storage
- collectArtifacts(assetsDir, repo, tag) -> map[string]Artifact — scan assets dir, read .sha256 files
- parseSHA256(raw) -> string — validate hex SHA256 from checksum file

Where to put it: New crate or an existing tool. Either:
- crates/looper-release/ as a library + cli tool
- Or as part of the release CI script (not Rust, just shell)

Recommendation: Port as a Rust library so the version compatibility check (MinCliForDaemon, MinDaemonForCli) can be embedded in both looperd and looper-cli at compile time.

Dependencies:
- Uses looper-storage (CurrentSchemaVersion reads migration list)
- Called from release CI pipeline
- Output consumed by auto-upgrade check in looper-cli

title: [Phase 10] Cross-cutting: Observability, Error handling, Rate limiting, Security
description:
Cross-cutting concerns spanning all crates. These must be designed upfront but implemented incrementally with each crate.

Spec refs: module-observability-error-handling.md, module-security-concurrency.md, module-rate-limit-degradation.md

Observability:
- tracing crate ecosystem: tracing + tracing-subscriber + tracing-appender
- tracing::Span for request-scoped context, tracing::Instrument for async span propagation
- tracing-subscriber JSON format + env-filter
- Event log system with type-safe event types (enum, not string), correlation/causation IDs
- Optional metrics (feature-gated prometheus endpoint)

Error handling:
- Per-crate error types with thiserror
- Cross-crate error conversion via From impls
- FailureKind + FailureBoundary enums (from looper-types)
- Error propagation policy: never leak internals to API/CLI
- Error display: user-friendly vs debug-verbose

Rate limiting & degradation:
- RetryPolicy: max_attempts, base_delay, max_delay, multiplier, jitter
- Exponential backoff: baseDelay * 2^attempt capped at 300s
- Overload protection: max_concurrent_runs, slow lane for >=5 attempts
- Graceful degradation: what happens when GitHub down / git locked / osascript unavailable / disk full
- Circuit breaker consideration for GitHub API repeated failures

Security:
- Auth: Bearer token + localhost exception
- Config file permissions: check 0600, warn on world-readable
- Env sanitization: 16 unsafe Git vars stripped
- Shell: direct exec (no shell wrapper)
- Worktree safety: 7 path validation checks
- Secret lifecycle: LocalToken, NodeToken, JoinKey, AdminToken, WebhookSecret

Dep: ALL crates


**Go source reference (legacy):** Spans many files (legacy/internal/bootstrap/logger.go, legacy/internal/infra/github/errors.go, legacy/internal/api/handler.go, legacy/internal/loops/failureclass/failureclass.go)

title: [Phase 4] looper-service: Loop, Run, Project business logic
description:
Service layer orchestrating Loop, Run, and Project lifecycle operations. Core business logic.

**Spec refs:** module-service-layer.md, module-domain-state-machine.md (state transitions)

**Key types:**
- `LoopService` — CRUD + state transitions for loops
- `RunService` — CRUD + lifecycle for runs
- `ProjectService` — project management with discovery
- `ResumePolicy` enum (shared with looper-types)

**Behaviors:**
- `LoopService::create()` — validate unique active, alloc seq, event log
- `LoopService::transition_status()` — validate allowed transition, side effects per transition:
  - idle→queued: create queue item
  - running→paused: cancel active queue items
  - running→completed: cleanup, mark done
  - running→failed: set resume policy, event log
  - paused→queued: requeue items
  - Any→terminated: cancel ALL, cleanup, terminal
- `LoopService::pause()` / `terminate()` / `resume()`
- `RunService::start_run()` — atomic: validate loop → create run → validate step → event log
- `RunService::record_step()` — heartbeat + step validation
- `RunService::complete()` — finalize run, transition loop if needed
- OneRunningRunPerLoop enforcement
- `ProjectService::add_project()` — ID collision, repo detection, discover worktrees/PRs
- `ProjectService::remove_project()` — archive + terminate loops + cancel queue
- `ProjectService::sync_configured()` — reconcile config-defined projects with DB

**Dependencies:** looper-types, looper-storage, looper-config

**Go source reference (legacy):** legacy/internal/loops/service.go + legacy/internal/runs/service.go + legacy/internal/projects/service.go

title: [Phase 12] diffanchor: diff parsing and anchor validation
description:
Diff anchor parsing utility — used by GitHub gateway for review comment validation.

Spec refs: module3-github-gateway.md (diffanchor sections)

Key types: Index { ranges: Vec<Range> }, Anchor { path, line, side, start_line, start_side }, Range { path, side, start, end, excerpt, heading }, ValidationResult { valid, reason, location_text, quality_flagged }

Behaviors:
- parse(diff: &str) → Index — parse unified diff format into structured ranges
- Index::format_prompt_section(limit) — format ranges as prompt context
- Index::validate(anchor) → ValidationResult — check if anchor points to valid location in diff
- validate_top_level_location(body) → ValidationResult
- fallback_body/fallback_location — degraded behavior for invalid anchors
- normalize_side(side) → normalize LEFT/RIGHT

Dep: looper-types

**Go source reference (legacy):** legacy/internal/diffanchor/diffanchor.go (435 lines)

title: [Phase 8c] looper-infra: Bootstrap, runtime lifecycle, notifications
description:
Infrastructure crate — daemon bootstrap sequence, runtime assembly, shell execution, notifications, worktree cleanup.

Spec refs: module-recovery-infra.md, module-observability-error-handling.md, module-security-concurrency.md

Key types: Runtime (assembles all services), BootstrapOptions, Logger, NotificationGateway, WorktreeCleanupService

Behaviors:
- Bootstrap: LoadConfig → ValidateToolPaths → EnsureWritableDirs → CreateLogger → StartRuntime → WaitForShutdown
- Logger: tracing-subscriber with JSON format + env-filter, file rotation (10MB, 5 files)
- Shell: tokio::process::Command with timeout, bounded output (256KB), SIGTERM→SIGKILL escalation, process group isolation, unsafe env vars stripped
- Runtime: assemble scheduler, webhook, service layer, active execution registry
- Notifications: in-app (DB upsert + event log) + osascript (AppleScript, sound levels, throttling 60s window)
- WorktreeCleanup: background loop (initial 1min, then configurable interval), Plan() with retention/loop/queue checks, safety validation
- Shutdown ordering: signal → API drain (1s) → scheduler stop → agent kill → webhook stop → storage close
- PID file + file lock for single instance enforcement

Dep: looper-types, looper-config, looper-storage, looper-service, looper-scheduler, looper-runner

**Go source reference (legacy):** legacy/internal/bootstrap/ + runtime/runtime.go + infra/shell/ + infra/notify/ + worktreecleanup/

title: [Phase 9a] looperd: Daemon binary entry point
description:
Daemon binary — thin entry point that wires all crates together into a running daemon process.

**Spec refs:** module10-cli-daemon.md (daemon sections), module-security-concurrency.md (shutdown ordering)

**Key behaviors:**
- Bootstrap sequence: check --version/--help → load config → validate tool paths (git, gh, osascript) → ensure writable dirs → create logger → start runtime → wait for shutdown
- Signal handling: SIGINT/SIGTERM → graceful shutdown via CancellationToken
- Graceful shutdown order: API drain (1s timeout) → scheduler stop → agent kill → webhook stop → storage close
- Single instance: PID file + fs2 file lock on ~/.looper/looperd.lock
- Startup logging: ~/.looper/logs/startup/ directory, main log at ~/.looper/logs/looperd.log
- Daemon state: ~/.looper/looperd.state.json with PID, mode, supervisor info

**Dep:** looper-config, looper-infra, looper-storage, looper-service, looper-scheduler, looper-runner, looper-api, looper-webhook

**Go source reference (legacy):** legacy/cmd/looperd/main.go (744 lines)

title: [Phase 0] Scaffold: Cargo workspace + CI/CD foundation
description:
Create the Cargo workspace skeleton with workspace root Cargo.toml, rust-toolchain.toml, rustfmt.toml, clippy.toml, .gitignore, LICENSE (MIT), README.md, and .github/workflows/ci.yml + release.yml.

This is the foundation for ALL other crates. Must compile with `cargo check --workspace` even with empty crates.

**Spec ref:** LOOPER_RUST_DESIGN.md

**Subtasks:**
1. Create workspace Cargo.toml with 16 crate members, workspace deps (tokio, serde, clap, rusqlite, axum, git2, octocrab, etc.)
2. Create rust-toolchain.toml (stable, 4 targets: aarch64-apple-darwin, x86_64-apple-darwin, x86_64-unknown-linux-musl, aarch64-unknown-linux-musl)
3. Create rustfmt.toml + clippy.toml
4. Create 16 empty crate stubs (lib.rs / main.rs) so workspace compiles
5. Create .github/workflows/ci.yml (fmt + clippy + test, 3-OS matrix)
6. Create .github/workflows/release.yml (cross-compile 4 targets on v tag)
7. Create AGENTS.md with build/test/lint commands
8. Verify: `cargo check --workspace` passes

**Go source reference (legacy):** legacy/LOOPER_RUST_DESIGN.md (build commands, CI/CD workflow specs)
close_reason: Implemented workspace scaffold: Cargo.toml (16 crates), rust-toolchain.toml, rustfmt.toml, clippy.toml, 16 crate stubs, .github/workflows/{ci,release}.yml, AGENTS.md. Verified: cargo check --workspace, cargo test --workspace, cargo fmt --check all pass.

title: [Phase 5b] looper-git: Git worktree management
description:
Git worktree management crate. Handles the full lifecycle of git worktrees used by runners.

**Spec refs:** module4-git-worktree.md, module-worktree-safety.md

**Key types:**
- `GitGateway` struct (git_path, storage ref, clock)
- `WorktreeRecord` — persisted state (id, project_id, repo_path, branch, status, head_sha)
- `CreateWorktreeInput`, `PrepareWorktreeInput`, `CommitInput`, `PushInput`
- `CheckoutMode` enum (Branch, Detached)
- `WorktreeListEntry` — parsed from git porcelain

**Behaviors:**
- CreateWorktree: validate branch not protected, compute path, git worktree add (detached/branch/new-branch), get HEAD, upsert DB record
- RestoreWorktree: lookup DB record, check health via git status, verify checkout mode matches
- CleanupWorktree: git worktree remove --force, mark "cleaned" in DB
- PrepareWorktree: fetch remote, reset --hard if clean, report head SHA
- InspectHead: git rev-parse HEAD, git rev-list for new commits, git status --porcelain for changed files
- Commit: git add -A + git commit -m with disclosure-stamped message
- Push: --force-with-lease with remote head validation, or simple push -u
- Branch naming: "looper/planner/{issue}-{slug}", "looper/worker/{slug}-{hash}", "looper-fix-{project}-pr-{N}"
- Sanitize branch names: keep [a-zA-Z0-9._-], replace rest with dash
- Safety: assert_writable_branch() at mutation points, 7 path validation checks (worktree-safety)
- Fetch ref lock retry: 2 retries, 50-100ms delays
- Remote head detection on push conflict

**Dependencies:** looper-types, looper-storage

**Go source reference (legacy):** legacy/internal/infra/git/gateway.go (1169 lines) + legacy/internal/worktreesafety/safety.go

title: Phase 13d — Review criteria verifier + auto-merge decision engine
description:
Port two reviewer sub-systems from Go.

PART 1: Review Criteria Verifier (internal/reviewer/criteria/ — 357 LOC)
Extracts acceptance criteria from issue body (looks for markdown checklist items, "Acceptance:" blocks, or numbered criteria). Then verifies each criterion against the PR diff using token matching.

Go source: legacy/internal/reviewer/criteria/criteria.go, default_verifier.go

Key types:
- AcceptanceCriterion — string wrapper
- Verdict: Pass, Fail, Unverifiable
- AggregateDisposition: Pass, Fail, Unverifiable
- Evidence: FilePath, StartLine, EndLine
- PRDiff: Files []DiffFile
- Verifier interface: VerifyCriterion(criterion, diff) -> CriterionAssessment
- CriterionAssessment: Verdict, Justification, Evidence
- VerificationResult: overall disposition + per-criterion assessments

Key functions:
- Extract(issueBody) -> []AcceptanceCriterion — parse criteria from markdown
- Verify(criteria, diff, verifier) -> VerificationResult — run all criteria
- DefaultVerifier.VerifyCriterion — token overlap matching across added lines

Where to put it: crates/looper-runner/src/reviewer_criteria.rs. Called from reviewer.rs during review execution.

PART 2: Auto-Merge Decision (internal/reviewer/automerge/ — 88 LOC)
Decides whether a PR qualifies for auto-merge. Checks branch protection, merge strategy compatibility, repo settings, and scope policy.

Go source: legacy/internal/reviewer/automerge/*.go

Key types:
- PRSnapshot: Labels, HasTrackedIssueLink
- BranchProtectionSnapshot: Exists, HasRequiredChecks
- RepoSettingsSnapshot: AllowSquashMerge, AllowMergeCommit, AllowRebaseMerge, AllowAutoMerge
- Config: Enabled, Strategy, RequireBranchProtection, TransientRetries, Scope
- AutoMergeDecision: OptIn(strategy), Refuse(reason)
- RefusalReason: Disabled, Scope, NoBranchProtection, StrategyDisallowed, AutoMergeDisabled

Where to put it: crates/looper-runner/src/automerge.rs. Called from merge_watch.rs during PR assessment.

Dependencies:
- Uses diffanchor for diff parsing (already exists)
- Uses looper-github gateway for PR/issue data
- Uses looper-types for domain types
- Part 2 uses looper-config for auto-merge settings

Test with: E2E test where fake-gh returns a PR with specific acceptance criteria in the linked issue, verifier produces correct pass/fail assessments.

title: [Phase 3b] looper-storage: Queue priorities + webhook state tables
description:
Queue priorities system and webhook forwarder/tunnel state storage — extension of looper-storage.

**Spec refs:** module2-storage-sqlite.md (queue_priorities), module8-api-webhook.md (webhook state)

**Key types:**
- `QueuePriority` constants (ordered: planner > reviewer > fixer > worker > snapshot)
- `QueueStats` struct (total_queued, eligible_queued, blocked_by_terminal_loop, blocked_by_lock_key, etc.)
- `QueueMarkRetryInput`, `QueueFailInput`
- `WebhookForwarderRecord`, `WebhookTunnelHookRecord`

**Behaviors:**
- Priority queue ordering in claim queries
- Long-term retry detection (attempts >= 5)
- Queue statistics computation
- Webhook forwarder CRUD (upsert, list, delete by repo)
- Tunnel hook management (ping tracking, orphan marking)
- Cleanup stale queued items

**Dependencies:** looper-types, looper-storage base

**Go source reference (legacy):** legacy/internal/storage/queue_priorities.go + webhook_forwarders.go + webhook_tunnel_hooks.go
close_reason: All Phase 3b functionality (QueuePriority, QueueStats, WebhookForwarderRecord, WebhookTunnelHookRecord, webhook CRUD, tunnel hook management, long-term retry detection, queue stats, cleanup) was already implemented as part of Phase 3a (looper-storage). No additional code needed.

title: [Phase 7] looper-runner: Planner, Reviewer, Fixer, Worker, Coordinator
description:
The LARGEST crate — 5 runner state machines. Each runner is a step pipeline with checkpoint/resume.

Spec refs: module7-runner.md, module-coordinator.md, module-reviewer-criteria.md

Key types per runner:
- Planner: steps discover-issues → prepare-worktree → write-spec → publish → notify
- Reviewer: steps discover → filter → claim → snapshot → review → publish (+ sub-ops: worktree prep, thread resolution)
- Fixer: steps discover-pr → claim-pr → collect-fixes → prepare-worktree → repair → validate → push → reconcile-commits → resolve-comments → recheck
- Worker: steps prepare-work → prepare-worktree → plan → execute → validate → open-pr
- Coordinator: tick-based (not step-based) — triage, dispatch, depgraph, mergewatch

Shared behaviors:
- Checkpoint/resume: JSON checkpoint persisted after each step, ResumePolicy controls next action
- Step execution: persistStepStarted() → executeStep() → classify failure or persistStepCompleted()
- Deduplication: FindActiveByDedupe → CreateOrGetActiveByDedupe
- Failure handling: classify → failQueueItem → completeRun → updateLoop
- Spec PR lifecycle: labels (looper:spec-reviewing, looper:spec-ready, looper:needs-human), phases (Spec vs Implementation)

Coordinator sub-systems:
- Triage: ShouldTriage/ShouldReTriage guards, LLM-based triage decision, label management
- Dispatch: human-gated (slash commands /plan, /implement) vs autonomous (delay-based), dependency gate
- DepGraph: blocked-by chains from GitHub API, cycle detection
- MergeWatch: 8 action types classification (Merged, Conflict, RedCI, etc.), PriorWatchMarker, retry budget

Reviewer criteria: AcceptanceCriterion, Verdict, Evidence, DefaultVerifier, AutoMerge validation

Dep: looper-types, looper-config, looper-storage, looper-github, looper-git, looper-agent, looper-service, looper-scheduler

**Go source reference (legacy):** legacy/internal/planner/ + reviewer/ + fixer/ + worker/ + coordinator/

title: Phase 13e — Dependency graph engine for multi-issue coordination
description:
Port the Go dependency graph engine from internal/coordinator/depgraph/ (342 LOC).

This builds a DAG from GitHub issue dependency relationships. It determines which issues are ready to work on (all deps satisfied), which are blocked, and whether any dependency cycles exist.

Go source: legacy/internal/coordinator/depgraph/depgraph.go

Key types:
- IssueRef: Repo + Number
- IssueState: State + StateReason
- Snapshot: BlockedBy map, Issues map, Unreachable refs
- Blocker: Issue, State, StateReason, Satisfied, RequiresReTriage, Unreachable
- Cycle: []IssueRef
- DependencyGraph: readySet, cycles, unreachable, blockers

Key functions:
- Build(tracked, snapshot) -> DependencyGraph — main entry from list of tracked issues
- graph.ReadySet() -> issues that can be worked on RIGHT NOW
- graph.Cycles() -> cycles in the dependency structure
- graph.Blockers(issue) -> what is blocking a specific issue
- graph.Unreachable() -> issues that reference non-existent issues
- detectCycles(tracked, edges) -> cycle detection algorithm
- normalizeBlockedBy / normalizeIssueStates — data normalization

Where to put it: crates/looper-runner/src/depgraph.rs. Called from coordinator.rs during discovery phase before dispatching to roles.

Integration: When coordinator discovers all tracked issues for a repo, it builds the dependency graph, finds the ready set, and only dispatches ready issues to their roles.

Dependencies:
- Uses looper-github gateway for list_issue_blocked_by, list_blocked_by_issues
- Uses looper-types for IssueRef
- Test can use fake-gh to return mock dependency relationships

Note: The Go code also has a "blocker" concept that integrates with queue dequeue to prevent blocked items from being claimed. This SQL-level blocking already exists in looper-storage/src/repos/queue.rs (blocked_by_terminal_or_paused_loop, blocked_by_lock_key). The depgraph integrates at the GitHub issue level (before queue).

title: Phase 13c — Triage engine: LLM-powered issue classification (valid/out-of-scope/unclear)
description:
Port the Go triage engine from internal/coordinator/triage/ (340+ Go LOC).

This runs when an issue gets labeled "looper:plan". It sends the issue title + body + repo context to an LLM, parses the structured JSON response, and applies labels plus a triage comment.

Go source: legacy/internal/coordinator/triage/triage.go

Key types to port:
- Disposition enum: Valid, OutOfScope, Unclear
- Issue struct: number, title, body, url, author, createdAt, labels, comments, timeline
- RepoContext: repo name, working directory, relevant paths, relevant symbols
- Config: triagedLabel, maxIssueAgeDays, maxPerTick, outOfScopeLabel, unclearLabel, reTriageOnAuthorReply
- Input = Issue + RepoContext + Config + Now
- LLM interface: Complete(ctx, Request) -> string
- Decision: NoOp, Disposition, label changes, comment body, markTriaged

Key functions:
- Decide(ctx, LLM, Input) -> Decision — main entry
- BuildPrompt(Input) -> string — structured LLM prompt
- parseDecision(raw, config) -> Decision — parse LLM JSON response
- ShouldTriage(Issue, Config, now) -> bool — is this issue eligible?
- ShouldReTriage(Issue, Config, now) -> bool — did author reply to "unclear"?
- SearchTokens(Issue) -> []string — extract tokens for repo context
- AllowedKinds(), AllowedAreas(), AllowedComplexities(), AllowedDispatches() -> label enums

Where to put it: crates/looper-runner/src/triage.rs, and add a TriageRole variant to dispatcher.
The LLM agent integration goes through looper-agent (ConfiguredExecutor or the existing agent gateway).

Integration: When scheduler discovers an issue with "looper:plan" label, coordinator calls triage before dispatching to planner. Triage result (labels + comment) is applied via looper-github gateway.

Dependencies:
- Uses looper-agent for LLM calls via ConfiguredExecutor
- Uses looper-github gateway to apply labels/comments
- Uses looper-types for domain types
- Uses looper-config for runtime settings

Test with: E2E test where fake-gh returns a labeled issue, triage engine calls fake-agent with structured prompt, and verifies correct label application.

title: [Phase 11] linter: cargo clippy fixes across workspace
description:
Final workspace-wide lint pass — ensure zero clippy warnings before release.

**Spec refs:** LOOPER_RUST_DESIGN.md (build commands section)

**Subtasks:**
1. cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings to list ALL warnings
2. Auto-fix: cargo clippy --fix --workspace --allow-dirty
3. Manual fix for remaining warnings (naming conventions, dead code, unnecessary casts, etc.)
4. cargo fmt after all fixes
5. Final verification: cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings passes with zero warnings
6. Verify cargo test --workspace still passes after fixes

**Note:** Run as the final step before release. Not needed during development — but run periodically to prevent warning accumulation.

**Dependencies:** All crates must exist (depends on looper_rust-e4b for workspace existence)


**Go source reference (legacy):** legacy/ (entire workspace, cargo clippy checks all crates)

title: [Phase 8b] looper-webhook: Webhook forwarder, event routing, tunnel
description:
Webhook forwarder receiving GitHub webhooks and routing to appropriate runner lanes.

Spec refs: module8-api-webhook.md (webhook sections), module-edge-cases.md (EC-021, EC-023, EC-072)

Key types: WebhookForwarder (queue with cap 128, 4 workers, TTL 1h), WebhookWorkItem, DeliveryStats

Behaviors:
- Event routing: pull_request → reviewer/fixer lanes based on action; pull_request_review → fixer; check_run → fixer; push → fixer as base_branch; issue_comment → ignore
- Routing logic: routeDelivery() — event type + action → lane(s)
- Project matching: case-insensitive FullName match
- Deduplication: delivery ID dedup (1h TTL) + work key dedup
- Worker pool: default 4 concurrent, retry up to 2x on transient errors
- Webhook lifecycle: startup (acquire daemon lock), reconciliation, shutdown (drain)
- Tunnel webhook management (webhook_tunnel_hooks table)
- Stats: deliveriesReceived, deduped, ignored, accepted, enqueued, coalesced, executions

Dep: looper-types, looper-config

**Go source reference (legacy):** legacy/internal/webhookforward/forwarder.go (753 lines)

title: Phase 13b — Runtime recovery pipeline: 5-phase startup recovery + webhook forwarder lifecycle
description:
Port the critical runtime recovery system from Go internal/runtime/runtime.go (~8K LOC). This is the self-healing system that runs when looperd starts up and keeps webhook forwarders alive.

Components to port:

1. Recovery Pipeline (runtime.go — 5 phases on startup):
   Phase 1 — Orphan agent cleanup: scan for agent processes whose parent daemon died. Verify PID still exists via ps. Kill orphans.
   Phase 2 — Expired lock release: find stale daemon locks, release them.
   Phase 3 — Stale run reconciliation: runs with heartbeat >30min old. Mark as interrupted, write recovery events.
   Phase 4 — Loop state normalization: requeue loops that had no active run.
   Phase 5 — Reviewer auto-recovery: deferred background check for orphaned review threads.

2. Webhook Forwarder Process Management (webhook_forwarder.go):
   - Start gh webhook forwarder for each configured repo
   - Classify exit codes as transient (respawn) or terminal (stop trying)
   - Stderr tail capture for diagnostics (last 20 lines)
   - Forwarder fingerprinting for dedup
   - Graceful shutdown with SIGTERM timeout

3. Webhook Tunnel Management (webhook_tunnel.go + webhook_lifecycle.go):
   - Create GitHub webhooks via gh CLI API
   - Update/delete hooks on config change
   - HMAC secret generation and auth
   - Disable latch: auto-disable after N consecutive failures in a rolling window
   - Forwarder status reporting for CLI

Where to put it: Either extend looper-infra (add recovery/ module) or extend looperd/src/main.rs with the recovery pipeline. The forwarder lifecycle should go in looper-webhook.

Dependencies:
- Uses looper-storage repos (events, locks, runs, loops, webhook_forwarders)
- Uses looper-github gateway for gh CLI access
- Uses looper-config for timeout/retry settings
- Must integrate with looperd bootstrap flow
- Requires Phase 13a (E2E tests) to verify

title: [Phase 9b] looper-cli: CLI binary, 35+ subcommands
description:
CLI binary — communicates with looperd daemon via REST API. Supports 35+ subcommands with JSON and human-readable output.

**Spec refs:** module10-cli-daemon.md (CLI sections), module-testing-strategy.md (snapshot tests)

**Key types:**
- `DaemonAPIClient` — HTTP client wrapping /api/v1/* endpoints with Envelope parsing, Bearer token auth
- Output formatters: human-readable (tables, key-value sections) + JSON (--json global flag)

**Command tree (35+ subcommands):**
- Query: status, version, ps (list loops), pr (list/show/status), project (list/add/remove), config (get/set/unset/show/edit/validate/migrate), loop (list/inspect/start/pause/retry), run (list/stats/reconcile-stale), queue (stats/list/failed/cleanup), event log
- Action: review (submit/repair), fix, work, plan, takeover (list/stop), stop, close, retry, pause/unpause, jump, logs (follow/--tail), feedback
- Daemon: daemon (install/start/stop/restart/logs/status), upgrade (--check/--cli/--daemon), bootstrap
- Admin: network (join/leave/status/members), webhook (enable/disable/status/cleanup/rotate), netadmin (onboard-repo/offboard-repo/repo-status), labels init, prompt preview, worktree cleanup

**Output modes:** human (tables) + JSON (--json). JSON mode has consistent Envelope format.
**Auth:** Bearer token from config, sent as Authorization header.

**Dep:** looper-types, looper-config

**Go source reference (legacy):** legacy/cmd/looper/main.go + legacy/internal/cliapp/ (50 files)

title: Phase 13g — Webhook tunnel server: gh hook management + HMAC auth + disable latch
description:
Port the webhook tunnel system from Go runtime (webhook_tunnel.go + webhook_lifecycle.go, ~1.5K LOC combined).

This creates and manages real GitHub webhooks via the gh CLI, so the looper daemon behind NAT can receive GitHub events. Currently Rust only has tunnel health check (67 LOC in tunnel.rs) — no actual hook management.

Go source: legacy/internal/runtime/webhook_tunnel.go, webhook_lifecycle.go

Key types:
- webhookTunnelGitHubHook: ID, Active, Events, Config (URL, ContentType, InsecureSSL), LastResponse
- webhookTunnelGitHubClient interface: GetHook, CreateHook, UpdateHook, DeleteHook
- ghWebhookTunnelClient: wraps gh CLI to manage hooks

Key functions:
- CreateHook(ctx, repo, url, secret, events) -> hook — create webhook via gh api
- GetHook(ctx, repo, id) -> hook, exists, error
- UpdateHook(ctx, repo, id, url, secret, events, active) -> hook
- DeleteHook(ctx, repo, id) -> error
- Tunnel secret: HMAC-SHA256 based on daemon auth token
- Disable latch: after N consecutive failures in rolling 24h window, auto-disable
- Forwarder exit classification: parse stderr for terminal vs transient errors
- Command fingerprinting for dedup of forwarder instances

Where to put it: crates/looper-webhook/src/tunnel.rs (expand current 67 LOC stub) + crates/looper-infra/src/recovery/ (for the disable latch + process lifecycle parts).

Integration: looperd calls webhook tunnel setup during bootstrap when network mode is not standalone. CLI queries tunnel status via looper-api (endpoints already exist).

Security:
- HMAC secret derivation from daemon token + repo path
- TLS required for production hook URLs
- Content type: json
- Events: pull_request, issue_comment, pull_request_review, pull_request_review_comment, push, check_run

Dependencies:
- Uses looper-github gateway for gh CLI execution
- Uses looper-storage repos (webhook_tunnel_hooks, webhook_forwarders)
- Uses looper-config for webhook settings and tunnel config
- Test with E2E harness fake-gh

This is a complex port but structurally straightforward — most of the Go code is gh CLI JSON parsing + HTTP.

title: [Phase 5c] looper-agent: Agent executor (5 vendors)
description:
Agent executor crate — manages running AI coding agents (Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode, Cursor CLI, Hermes).

**Spec refs:** module5-agent-executor.md, module-edge-cases.md (EC-030 to EC-036)

**Key types:**
- `ExecutorConfig` — vendor, model, params, env map, native_resume_enabled
- `RunInput` — execution_id, prompt, working_directory, timeout, heartbeat_timeout, native_session_id
- `ExecutionResult` — status (completed/failed/timeout/killed), summary, stdout/stderr, parse_status, completed_files, changed_files, commits, pid
- `AgentExecutionRecord` — persisted (status: running, cancelling, completed, failed, interrupted)
- `StreamCapture` — bounded output buffer 256KB in-memory + full log file

**Behaviors:**
- Vendor-specific arg construction (exact per-vendor flags with --model, --print, --dangerously-skip-permissions):
  - claude-code: "claude --model {} --print {} --dangerously-skip-permissions"
  - codex: "codex exec --model {} {}"  (resume: "exec resume {} {}")
  - opencode: "opencode run --model {} --dir {} {}"  (resume: "--session {}")
  - cursor-cli: "agent --model {} --print {}"  (resume: "--resume {}")
  - hermes: "hermes -m {} -z {}" (no native resume)
- Native resume: per-vendor flag patterns, session ID persistence
- Two-tier timeout: max_runtime timer + idle/heartbeat timer, configurable per runner
- Process group kill: SIGTERM + 5s grace + SIGKILL escalation (Unix); Job Object API (Windows)
- Completion marker: scan stdout+stderr for "__LOOPER_RESULT__={\"summary\":\"...\"}" (reverse line order)
- Env injection: LOOPER_PROMPT, LOOPER_COMPLETION_MARKER, unsafe git env vars stripped (16 keys)
- Native session ID extraction from output (JSON keys: nativeSessionId, sessionId, chatId)
- Log persistence: "{logDir}/loops/{loopID}/{runID}/{executionID}.{stdout,stderr}.log"

**Dependencies:** looper-types, looper-config, looper-storage

**Go source reference (legacy):** legacy/internal/agent/executor.go (1737 lines) + prompt.go

title: [Phase 3a] looper-storage: SQLite repositories, migrations, event log
description:
SQLite storage layer with 17 migrations, repository pattern, event log, and queue operations.

**Spec refs:** module2-storage-sqlite.md, module-edge-cases.md (EC-001 to EC-006)

**Key types:**
- `Repositories` container — all repo structs
- Individual repos: Projects, Loops, Runs, AgentExecutions, PRSnapshots, Events, Locks, Queue, Notifications, Worktrees, WebhookForwarders, WebhookTunnelHooks
- `MigrationRunner` — apply 17 embedded migrations via refinery
- `QueueItemRecord`, `ProjectRecord`, `LoopRecord`, `RunRecord`, `EventLogRecord`, `LockRecord`, etc.

**Behaviors:**
- Connection management: WAL mode, busy_timeout=5000ms, `_txlock=immediate`, pool size 4
- Migration runner: forward-only, embedded SQL files, schema version tracking
- Queue claim: CTE `UPDATE ... RETURNING` for atomic claim
- Lock acquire: `INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE WHERE expires_at <= now`
- Event log: append with correlation_id, causation_id, actor tracking
- OneRunningRunPerLoop: partial unique index enforcement
- Queue dedup: unique partial index on dedupe_key WHERE status IN (queued,running)
- `WithTransaction` / `WithTransactionValue` helpers for atomic operations

**Dependencies:** looper-types, looper-config (for StorageConfig)

**Edge cases:** SQLITE_BUSY retry, migration version mismatch, ID collision, concurrent writes, large data growth

**Tests:** Migration sequence, repository CRUD, queue priority ordering, lock acquire/release, concurrent writes

**Go source reference (legacy):** legacy/internal/storage/repositories.go + legacy/internal/storage/migrations/ (17 files)

title: [Phase 9c] looper-net: Loopernet cloud server binary
description:
Loopernet cloud server binary — multi-node coordination server for distributed looper deployments.

**Spec refs:** module9-network.md, module-rate-limit-degradation.md (network partition handling)

**Key types:**
- `CloudServer` — axum HTTP server with SQLite backend
- `NodeRecord` — node_id, node_name, node_token, daemon_version, github identity, capabilities, heartbeat
- `CoordinatorLease` — single "coordinator" lease per network, 30s TTL, fencing token
- `JoinKey` — one-time use, stored in SQLite, consumed atomically in transaction
- `NodeCapabilities` — roles, coordinator_eligible, routed_projects, reviewer_projects, local_projects, dynamic_load, identity_drift
- `NetworkClient` — client-side HTTP wrapper (join, heartbeat, leave, lease ops)
- `Manager` — heartbeat loop (10s interval), state persistence in ~/.looper/network.json (0600)
- `LocalState` — persisted node state (url, network_id, node_id, node_token, github identity)
- `ClaimDecision`, `ProjectPolicy` — work distribution policy

**Endpoints (14):** POST /v1/join, /v1/heartbeat, /v1/leave, /v1/coordinator-lease/{acquire,renew,handoff,expire,revalidate}, GET /v1/status, /v1/events (SSE), GET /v1/github/webhook-secret, POST /v1/github/webhook (HMAC auth), GET/POST admin routes

**Auth tiers:** AdminToken (env LOOPERNET_ADMIN_TOKEN) + NodeToken (random at join)
**Lease:** fencing token prevents split-brain, revalidation endpoint for stale lease detection
**Duplicate detection:** warns on duplicate GitHub numeric IDs across nodes

**Dep:** looper-types, looper-config

**Go source reference (legacy):** legacy/internal/network/ (client/ + cloud/ + protocol/ + networkpolicy/)

title: Phase 13f — Lifecycle policy: agent-vs-human action tracking
description:
Port the Go lifecycle policy from internal/lifecycle/ (474 LOC).

This tracks who is responsible for each action in a run: was a commit made by an AI agent or by a human fallback? This is crucial for audit, observability, and determining how much autonomy the system actually has.

Go source: legacy/internal/lifecycle/lifecycle.go

Key types:
- Policy: Name, Version, ExpectCommit, ExpectPush, ExpectPR, AllowFallback, EligibleRunners
- Actions: Commit(string), Push(string), PR(string) — source per action (none/agent/fallback)
- State: Policy, Branch, BaseBranch, PlannedBranch/BaseBranch, AgentBranch/BaseBranch, ActiveBranch/BaseBranch, BranchProvenance (planned/agent_migrated), CommitSHAs, Pushed, PRNumber, PRURL, PRAdopted, Actions, ReconciledAt, ReconciledBy, LastError, AgentIngestedAt

Key functions:
- AgentManagedWithFallbackPolicy(runner, expectPR) -> Policy — default policy for all runner roles
- NewState(policy, branch, baseBranch) -> State — create initial lifecycle state
- State.UnmarshalJSON — handles both snake_case and camelCase JSON fields
- parseActionSource -> none/agent/fallback
- BranchProvenance constants: Planned, AgentMigrated

Where to put it: crates/looper-runner/src/lifecycle.rs. Each run gets a LifecycleState that the fixer/worker updates as they make commits/pushes/PRs.

Integration: When Fixer or Worker pushes a commit, they update the lifecycle state with the commit SHA and action source (agent). When Planner creates a PR, they set PRNumber and PRAdopted. The coordinator reads lifecycle during recovery to determine if a run was agent-driven.

Dependencies:
- Uses looper-types for domain types
- Serialized/deserialized into SQLite as JSON via looper-storage run record
- Config setting: enable lifecycle tracking (default: true)

Test with: Unit tests for state transitions, JSON roundtrip, and action source parsing.

title: Phase 13h — CLI parity: 15 missing command groups (review submit, takeover, logs follow, daemon, upgrade, webhook, etc.)
description:
Port the 15 missing CLI command groups from Go cliapp/ (~8K LOC) to Rust.

Go source: legacy/internal/cliapp/

Commands to port (ordered by priority):

HIGH PRIORITY:
1. review submit (394 LOC Go) — Submit a review manually for a specific PR. User provides inline comments, the system normalizes anchors (via diffanchor), runs quality gate, and posts via gh API.
   - Rust equivalent: extend crates/looper-cli/src/commands/ with review.rs
   - Uses looper-github gateway (submit_review) + diffanchor

2. run takeover (993 LOC Go) — Take over an agent run. User can claim/relinquish control of a running agent session. Handles agent PID transfer, session state update, and notification.
   - Rust: new crates/looper-cli/src/commands/takeover.rs
   - Uses looper-service (get/update run) + looper-agent (session resume)

3. run stats (452 LOC Go) — Run statistics and timeline. Shows elapsed time, agent calls count, failure history, state transitions.
   - Rust: extend runs.rs command with stats subcommand
   - Uses looper-storage event log

4. logs follow (200 LOC Go) — SSE-stream agent logs for a specific run in real-time. Connects to daemon SSE endpoint.
   - Rust: extend runs.rs with follow subcommand
   - Uses looper-api SSE endpoint

MEDIUM PRIORITY:
5. daemon install (319 LOC Go) — Install daemon as systemd/launchd service
6. daemon supervision (552 LOC Go) — Daemon supervision management (restart policies, health thresholds)
7. daemon runtime (1496 LOC Go) — Runtime health: version compatibility check between CLI and daemon, migration status
8. network admin (231 LOC Go) — Network admin commands (node management, cluster status)
9. labels init (114 LOC Go) — Initialize standard looper labels on a repo
10. prompt (232 LOC Go) — Prompt subcommand (interactive prompt for agent)
11. feedback (159 LOC Go) — User feedback prompts (ratings, bug reports)

LOWER PRIORITY:
12. upgrade (1588 LOC Go) — Full self-upgrade (Rust has basic version check only in autoupgrade.rs — needs the full upgrade flow)
13. webhook group (928 LOC Go) — Manage forwarders and tunnels (list, create, delete, status, logs)
14. loop diagnostics (817 LOC Go) — Loop diagnostics (state graph, timeline, event log dump)
15. progress (277 LOC Go) — Progress display (animated terminal output during runs)

Dependencies:
- Each command depends on looper-cli infrastructure (client, output, error types)
- Most call looper-api endpoints on the daemon
- Some directly use looper-github (labels init, review submit)
- Test with E2E harness

Where to put it: extend crates/looper-cli/src/commands/ with new modules, add variants to Cli enum in main.rs.
